reviews.review_title: Would love to give it five stars but....
reviews.review_description:
Would love to give it five stars but....This game needs  alot of work in the physics department. Lets start off with the gun handling is autrocious, 5/10 times you miss grab when trying to aim. Lets not get started on your equipped items, these you equip before a level starts and you will only be allowed to use two weapons when you clearly have three or four equipped. BTW if  a zombie gets too close, you can forget about killing at point blank range because the bullets pass right through....

After a few more hours you realise that that the upgrading system is very unbalanced. YOu will spend thousands upgrading a weapon a few levels only to have it do even less damage than a weapon in the shop thats at a lower level! I can't make this up, play it and find out. Dev's fix this game. You need a model game to map to? Play and finish one of the Zero Caliber games. The physics are correct, the upgrading is very balanced the weapon handling is smooth and articulating unlike this game which has mediocore sound effects that try to make up for very poor handling. I'm going to uninstall and check back in a few months.

I just found my vr controllers and wanted to start playing. I saw this game and took a chance and bought it ,surprised me I love this game.i love the story ,game play weapons,leveling system.i wish there was a updated and on to buy more content ,I keep replaying the levels,this is a must buy.

The game has some defect. First of all I found the "handling" of the objects a little too much trouble and confusing, 2: the adjust Holster system don't work (Meta Quest 3S controllers compatibility?). 3: the upgrade system and how to apply the various parts, too complicated and dispersive (sincerely there I still understand anything). 4: he talks about being able to lower himself but there is no way to do it and therefore it is impossible to reach objects on the ground. 5: in the trailer the character has his arms, while in the game he has floating hands. Too bad, because adjusting these things the game would be good enough.

The game is fun and I enjoy it however the trailer is a little misleading. I was disappointed when I launched it and found out you could not have arms and you could only have floating hands unlike the trailer where they had arms.

been looking for a space game suvival shooter, bought this cause i needed another game to work on and looked like it might turn out descent. it gets pretty fun as you progress, plays like an arcade shooter with zombies, firefights, robots and other monsters. guns get pretty awesome as you upgrade them. wish you could grip the rifle by holding the barrel instead of clicking it . good constant action
It's a great game but it's clunky and the movement needs work while I was playing it would often change forward movement to backwards movement while I was in a fight
Brilliant game. I just hope  devs add moore on to  this lovely game .it was so good 👍 well done

Dead Effect 2 VR is primarily a role-playing game. At the game's outset, you choose between three different characters with varying abilities.

As the action unfolds, you're thrust into a single-player campaign set in a semi-destroyed spaceship, tasked with completing simple missions to progress. This ranges from rescuing survivors to fixing issues with the ship's oxygen systems.

With over 30 weapons at your disposal, you can navigate your way through the ruined spaceship. From pistols to shotguns, plasma rifles, bows, swords, hammers, grenades, and sniper rifles – all weapons are not only upgradeable but also available at different levels.

reviews.review_title: My favorite game so far and a must play
reviews.review_description:
I was looking for a game for my Quest 3 that I would enjoy. I have bought many lately and some were good, but none that gave me the experience like Halo, Doom 2, Dead Space, or Star Craft gave me the first time I played it. Well, I think this is the one. So far, I like everything about it. It is an awesome experience playing this in VR. The graphics, although not as good as Red Matter 2, still, it is "very good" and enjoyable.  This Game is a Must Play. I love space marine style FPS games and this is all that. What is so disturbing is that it was so hard to find. It does not show up in the Meta Quest store unless you know the name and type it in to search. Also, reviewers who post on "you tube" passed this by or missed it somehow. This game needs to be experience. So, Developers, you need to get this game more face time, or advertisement so people won't have such a hard time finding it.  Kudos to the developers of this game, well done. 
Another note, I also love that this is not a short game like the Vader episodes. It has depth and love the level up system for your character and equipment. 
Also, I have not experienced any bugs or discomfort issues in the 10+ hours I played it so far.
I beat Doom 1,2, 3, RTCW, Half-Life w/ expansions, and I'm definitely going to play this until the end. Release part 1, I'll buy it.

reviews.review_title: Good game for app lab BUT BUGGY
reviews.review_description: After playing it for some  hours, ihave to say   the game is ok but very confusing.  There is no challenge because you can respawn instantly in the same place when you die. Shooting with pistols is good but if you hold a shotgun or a rifle with 2 hands the second hand is always moving  on the gun(fix it devs). That hand bug is a NO GO in a shooter . Could be a great game without this bug.
https://www.oculus. com/appreferrals/justin.isafake/3767727833315520/?utm_source=4
You won't be sorry for buying.
I hate games that are confusing from the start for no reason

I was looking for a full length game on the quest and this is it the gameplay is a mix of doom/dead space with so much to do character/weapon customisation abilities to upgrade and the immersive gameplay is just top notch and the one thing I personally love is that it has loads of oculus scoreboard achievements to earn which I feel is lacking in so many other games, bottom line this game is a five star rating and is worth the price, I would recommend this game instantly and a must buy for any quest owner.
I hope that a dead effect 3 vr will release someday or maybe more updates/content for this one (with more achievements lol)

reviews.review_title: Great game with lots of content
reviews.review_description: Really fun game with cool story and lots of content, one of the best zombie shooter's of all.

I’ve played this series in the mobile incarnation and have always thought it was at the top of that category. So of course I was super excited when I found this in the app lab! One of the few games I’ve bought immediately.

If you’re looking for a LOADED single player experience, I cannot recommend this enough. I’ve often wondered how they fit everything into this game. There’s the campaign, which is divided into sections. I’m halfway through pt. 2 and have already put in probably 10 hours. Part 1 was like 12 or 15 stages. There’s a dedicated side quest menu. There’s lone wolf missions. There’s survival missions. There’s wave missions…. There’s just so much to do it’s unbelievable that it’s a native oculus title.

Now let’s talk about the RPG elements. You’ve got upgradable weapons and armor pieces. You’ve got upgradable attachments for all those as well. You’ve got upgradable skills. You’ve got upgradable and unlockable abilities. You’re constantly finding better weapons and gear as you play… it’s just ridiculous the amount of RPG elements in this and people don’t mention it when looking for RPG titles (which is severely lacking on the native oculus). 

Absolutely the best $20 bucks I’ve spent on a game this far. I’ve got probably 30 games. This is one of the 4 I’m constantly coming back to. If you’re looking for the most underrated title in the App Store, you’ve found it! If I had to find one grievance I’d have to say the melee combat could feel better. 

If you’re on the fence, I guarantee you will not be disappointed in this game. Until a true RPG title is released, this scratches the itch nicely and shows what’s possible. Combine that with zombie killin’…. C’mon man!

I probably put in about 25 hours into this game but had to swap out my headset due to hardware failure. When I installed the game to the new headset my heart skipped a beat when 'continue' wasn't an option and I had to start a 'new game'. 

Had a lot of fun but I don't know if I'm willing to start all over again.

reviews.review_title: One of the best shooters on the quest
reviews.review_description: I’m a huge fan of first person shooters , it’s one of my favorite genres , we have a ton of shooters on the quest platform , but not a lot of them offer a full single player campaign that’s more than a couple of hours long, but this one does , the campaign is over 8 hrs long , and not based on online multiplayer arena gameplay , this game is for the hardcore old school gamers that enjoy a single player experience, and the graphics are some of the best on the quest 2! It’s not a perfect game , but it’s awesome , I strongly recommend this game to anyone looking for a good single player game !!

I personally find the game super fun! Without the coop option that exists on other platforms I cant even convince other people to check it out. Please consider adding that option I think you will find that your sales will expand for that reason alone!  I will change my rating to 5 when you do.

reviews.review_title: proper full story game with lots to do
reviews.review_description:
this is a solid game with some b tier voice acting. 

Dead effect 2 is a vr port of a vr port of a mobile game but do not let that put you off. visuals take a bit of a hit from pcvr as expected but the core experience remains. 

I am not sure if coop made it in. I can only hope that if it is not in then bad fly are working on it.

a lot of fun to be had with dead effect 2 at a good price. dont miss out of this rough diamond.

Hello loving the game so far, been playing the game since mobile days and I love that the game is now on the quest. I gave 4 stars because I can't seem to customize my gun holsters like in the pc version. I'm not sure if it's a bug, it says touch the holsters with your controller and drag it to the area where you want but it doesn't work. Hopefully they will fix this issue soon.

I really enjoy the game. It has the right feel to it and playing with the shotgun really works for me the manual pump action and pover of it against zombies :) amazing. I don't know how the game will go from here but more than 2h in and can't wait for Oculus 2 to charge to jump straigh back :)

I'm having fun with this game so far but I've had a few issues with the controls. I had to turn crouch off because it would just randomly make me crouch as I walked around, if it was bound to pushing down the right joystick that would be a lot better. Also when I set it to sitting position I cannot reach my pistol no matter what I do and I can't move or resize my holsters so I have to open the menu and select it to use it. There's not a lot of instructions on how to do any of that stuff either so you just kinda have to wing it which is a little annoying and time consuming. I hope the game improves because it's quite good once you figure out what you're doing.

this game has almost everything to please the gamplay is really immersive however some problems to report, it sparkles quite a bit on the graphics side without forgetting the gamplay despite having calibrated the height in the menu we never have the same size in the game and it really is too bad, hoping for a corrective patch soon which could surely hoist this game among the best in the catalog. A French version or at least French subtitles would be greatly appreciated, I would definitely put 5 stars thereafter
Very good hope they do a quest 2 upgrade

Zombie shooter game with many mods.  You can carry multiple weapons and upgrade them with different perks.  There are many different armors and such and lots of content.  I love how much stuff there is to do in the game.  I do hate the glitch where you can't finish a level because a zombie got blown off the map but didn't die.  Also wish there was coop, and that you need to heal each other to survive instead of just paying to respawn.
The story is kind of meh, but blowing off a zombie's legs with a shotgun then smashing its skull in with Mjolnir more than makes up for.
The game is Packed with Lots of interacting and scary moments!! You won't be disappointed!!
Just needs co op and it will be perfect it’s a great port probably one of the best!

Uhhh… when? I bought this game over 6 months ago, I read an article from the developers. It said multiplayer/co-op would be added, half the reason I bought it. They now have it on app lab  and there is still no word or anything about co-op or multiplayer. In fact I can’t even find the article anymore.. :( This is a cool game but for me personally it was a waste of money. I saw they added it on app lab and still no multiplayer… I searched the internet for the past two hours looking for an answer and there is none. I reinstall the game to find all my progress lost. At this point I would ask for a refund if I could, but I can’t. If you want multiplayer DO NOT BUY THIS!! If you want a 10 hour campaign from a cell phone game then go for it. At this point I’m deleting again and will not play until multiplayer of some sort is out, waste of 20 dollars in my opinion. It’s not like it would be hard to add, seeing as how it’s on the mobile version… Quest 2 is a cell phone with a 3D display, no reason multiplayer couldn’t be ported over. I hope the developers read this. When “After the fall” comes out you would have to pay me to play this, if multiplayer/coop comes out I’ll consider it. Otherwise every “zombie” game has another thing coming, and you’ll be left in the dust.

Hands down the best zombie RPG type shooter you got guns you got swords you got brass knuckles you got magic you got character leveling up you got armor leveling up you got weapons that constantly change in the store you got minikin who is awesome😁 love this game just wish it had Co-op and a forearm rig model update please would recommend this to everybody who likes zombie games and character leveling up and the storytelling is great it was meant for VR original Android port with cryo controls what is Magic this is dreams come true sooooooo much fun. Can't forget the massive amount of replay value multiple characters multiple game modes survival dude you want a game that you're going to get mad hours in and want to keep coming back to this is that great job guys👍👏

I played for a little bit tested all the characters and their weapons but the weapon sights and scopes are kinda wacky probably change the big green dot to a little red circle or something and kayne or kays katana is a little easy when fighting you just swing and there dead Maybe add weak spots and no insta kill but overall pretty good

reviews.review_title: A must play Doom/dead space -like game
reviews.review_description: I am pleasently surprised by this game. The level designs are super immerssive. I had been dreaming of quest 2 games inside giant spaceships, and this is it. This is a great pc port game, and we need lots more of this type.  Graphics and gameplay are awesome. Weapon handling and grabbing need a few minor but important tweaks. This is a MUST play/own game!
The game is awesome and very great but I just have one question. When will the multiplayer be out. If you see this review please tell me

reviews.review_title: One of the best games on Quest 2
reviews.review_description: After adjusting the settings to fit my style of play, it needs to be said that this is one of the best games on the Quest 2 platform. I do wish the games controls (height adjust, menu size, NPC size etc.) was already optimised, because it can be a bit of a fiddle, but once done, wow what a game this is. 🌟⚡💗 I didn't think I'd get to play a game with this amount of gameplay on the Quest 2. It's truly remarkable and has set a standard for what kind of games can be made for this platform. 9/10

I used to play this game on my phone a while back. It translates pretty well into VR. The 2 handed weapons are a little wonky, but not hard to get used to. If you are more interested in the story, I would just get the phone version. It'll save you 20 bucks.
Grade A gaming.

this is a great game but i can't get over the reloading mecanichs 
in the game.
it is just like arizona sunshine reloading mecanichs.
if you've played the game you now what i mean.
in the game you'll be shooting zombies and it is a really big game with a bunch of weapons and 
a bunch of missions to do.

i recomend this game if you like zombies or just having some fun

25% auf bio.link lordvalfromhell


And here it is! My absolute favorite game on the Quest! How come? While I have a lot of games coming right after it, and I'm generally more into games that offer co-op as I prefer to play with my buddies, Dead Effect 2 is the perfect single player experience for me. The game doesn't focus on the story, even if there is one, but rather on a wide range of weapons, many levels and a lot of shooting. Skill trees for the character and the weapons are manageable and do not complicate. The weapons feel powerful and every shot packs a punch. In addition to zombies and various monsters, you will also encounter human opponents again and again in the game, which means that the game offers a wide variety. Graphically, it's absolutely fine and can particularly convince with the effects. There are a lot of customization options and each mission can be played over and over again to improve your final rating. What still stands out next to the action are the small puzzles. These are sometimes a bit too easy, but they offer a change. A few more details should be mentioned that make the game a real immersive experience, so the NPCs doesn't let themselfs be groped and resists the attempts.
I'm totally hooked on the title and I've had over four hours of play now and I still can't see the end!
Thanks for this game!
